candidates.direction_name: Single-Source Warp Ownership End To End On The Half-Panel Branch
ranking_notes: This stays on top because the current run reproduced the same high-warps, low-scoreboard signature that justified reopening the family.
A one-round continuation is defensible because there is now local confirmation of the signal, not just historical memory.
If this targeted repair still fails, the family can be closed with much stronger evidence.
candidates.hypothesis: Round 22 confirmed that the half-panel branch still attacks the right wall on the current machine. The result was bad overall, but the machine state moved exactly where the plateaued 24.16-24.18 ms surfaces do not: active warps rose to 32.92%, register occupancy stayed at limit=2, and long scoreboard dropped to 0.62%. The remaining blockers are now very explicit: correctness failed 0/3 and barrier climbed to 21.27%. That makes the next best move a precise continuation inside the same family, not a fresh family jump. The historical 76622e3 snapshot is that continuation: it single-sources warp ownership and panel identity end to end so the branch can keep the occupancy breakthrough while attacking the remaining overlapping-writer / ownership mismatch.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Half-panel ownership and export correctness on top of a branch that already solved the live-state wall but still pays excessive synchronization.
candidates.risk: High. This branch still fails correctness and the next step is another broad historical surface restore, but now the key occupancy signal has been reproduced locally.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the 76622e3 continuation still fails correctness and does not materially reduce the branch's 21% barrier stall while preserving the occupancy breakthrough.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
ranking_notes: This remains the safest fallback after any aggressive branch failure.
It is not ranked first because the half-panel branch still has unfinished but now locally verified signal.
If dir_01 fails again, this should become the immediate next action.
candidates.hypothesis: If the half-panel continuation misses again, the correct immediate recovery is still the exact PTX best at 489574e. That surface remains the fastest measured implementation and the cleanest baseline for any later follow-on families.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: No new bottleneck is being attacked; this is an exact recovery of the best-known implementation surface.
candidates.risk: Low. This is the exact best measured surface.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop once the exact PTX winner surface is restored or if a remeasurement shows it no longer reproduces the 24.16-24.18 ms band.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface
ranking_notes: This keeps the current best non-PTX alternate surface live as a correct fallback.
It ranks below dir_02 because the PTX best is still the faster recovery target.
It is still useful because it preserves a second correct surface if the loop wants to avoid re-collapsing onto PTX immediately.
candidates.hypothesis: The current half-panel source is no longer a good safe surface. If the loop wants a correct non-PTX fallback without returning all the way to the PTX winner, the exact round-21 sibling surface at 910beff is the right recovery branch. It stayed inside the plateau band, remained correct, and preserved a different execution surface from the PTX microkernel.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: This is a safe alternate-surface recovery, not a new bottleneck attack.
candidates.risk: Low to medium. This is an exact restore of the current best non-PTX sibling surface.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop once the round-21 sibling surface is restored or if a remeasurement shows it no longer behaves like the prior 24.17 ms plateau alternate.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
